ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'french fries food product' class; curly fries are a shaped variant of deep-fried potato strips. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers fried potato products broadly; no specific curly fries sub-code exists. Closest defensible category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for curly fries or seasoned fried potato products. Null used to avoid fabrication.

allergens: may contain traces of milk and soy

serving.natural_language_portion: One large serving of curly fries at Wingstop Singapore is approximately 170 g, consistent with a standard fast-food large fries portion.

nutrient_source.title: Potato, french fried, all types, salt added in processing, frozen, as purchased (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 170697)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Curly fries are a seasoned, spiral-cut variant of deep-fried potato strips. USDA SR Legacy entry for frozen french fries (FDC ID 170697) was used as the primary nutrient anchor for core macros and secondary nutrients. Sodium was adjusted upward to reflect the heavier seasoning coating typical of curly fries (approximately 450–500 mg/100 g range reported in USDA Branded Food entries for seasoned curly fries, e.g., Arby's Curly Fries FDC ID 2103606). Energy, fat, and carbohydrate values are consistent with USDA Branded Food data for seasoned curly fries. Values represent a best-estimate composite from USDA SR Legacy and USDA Branded Food data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — French fries / fried potato
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for seasoned curly fries. GI of 75 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables, which report GI values of 63–75 for deep-fried potato products (french fries, fried potatoes). The upper value of 75 is used to reflect the additional starch coating and seasoning on curly fries, which may increase the glycemic response relative to plain fries. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 40.5 × 1.70) / 100 = 51.6.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg/100 g; ~816 mg per large serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid curly fries due to the high sodium content from seasoning coatings, as a single large serving can provide approximately 35–40% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (75) and high glycemic load per serving (~52)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Curly fries carry a high glycemic index of 75 and a glycemic load of approximately 52 per large serving, making them unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as they can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high total fat (~26 g per serving), saturated fat (~3.6 g), trace trans fat, and high sodium (~816 mg) in a large serving of curly fries presents multiple cardiovascular risk factors and should be consumed only occasionally by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Curly fries (large) are a popular fast-food snack consisting of spiral-cut, skin-on potato strips that are coated in a seasoned batter and deep-fried until golden and crispy. The spiral shape is achieved by cutting potatoes with a specialized helical blade, resulting in a distinctive corkscrew appearance. The seasoning blend typically includes paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and other spices, giving curly fries a more robust, spiced flavour compared to plain french fries. In Singapore, curly fries are prominently served at Wingstop outlets (e.g., City Square Mall and Funan) as a side dish accompanying chicken wings. A large serving weighs approximately 170 g and provides around 530 kcal, 26 g of fat, and 69 g of carbohydrates. The high glycemic index (approximately 75) and glycemic load (approximately 52 per large serving) make curly fries a high-glycemic food. The sodium content is elevated due to the seasoning coating (~816 mg per large serving), raising concerns for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Curly fries are classified as ultra-processed and are not suitable for frequent consumption in cardiometabolic health management. They are halal-certified at Wingstop Singapore and are suitable for vegetarians and vegans, though they contain gluten from the seasoning coating.
common_user_queries: How many calories are in Wingstop curly fries large?
Are curly fries halal in Singapore?
What is the GI of curly fries?
Are curly fries suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in curly fries?
What are the macros for large curly fries?
Is Wingstop curly fries gluten free?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for Wingstop Singapore curly fries was available from an approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy (FDC ID 170697) for frozen french fries and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food data for seasoned curly fries (e.g., Arby's Curly Fries FDC ID 2103606). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried potato products. Confidence is medium because the values are well-grounded in approved USDA sources but are not an exact match for this specific product.
notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 170697) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Food entries for seasoned curly fries. Sodium is set at 480 mg/100 g to reflect the heavier seasoning coating of curly fries versus plain fries. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA source entries used. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for fried potato products. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-derived food product with no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for potato-based fried products. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as curly fries are fried in vegetable oil with no animal-derived fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried potato products from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. Serving size of 170 g is estimated based on standard fast-food large fries portion conventions. Halal status confirmed from Wingstop Singapore cuisine tags in source data. Gluten allergen noted due to wheat starch in seasoning coating typical of commercial curly fries.
